title: Carbon Pipeline Eminent Domain Ban Advances to SD House; â€˜Compromiseâ€™ Bill Gutted
content: Seven committee members voted for South Dakota's House Bill 1052 , which will head to the Senate floor. The Bill would prohibit eminent domain use for carbon pipelines.  Senate Bill 198 was amended after the House State Affairs Committee voted unanimously to defeat the bill. The original legislation would have retained eminent domain as an option, but required entities using it to first attend mediation with the affected landowner and to also have a Public Utilities Commission permit before commencing eminent domain proceedings. The committee plans to reconsider the amended SB on Wednesday, after the Senate decides the fate of HB 1052.
author: Analysts Account
